wave0_rule: Claim source of truth = `git status` re-run at dispatch time; every dirty file is claimed by in-flight work; units listing a claimed file hard-gate on its land and begin with an explicit rebase-first step (re-read the seam; docket line numbers are untrusted).
integration_protocol: Per wave: merge unit branches in the stated order -> python scripts/regen_all.py --write ONCE -> ruff check --fix / ruff format / mypy src/hpc_agent -> targeted batteries incl. named -m slow legs -> push -> GitHub CI matrix green (Linux gates win32 skips) -> append the wave's enforcement-map rows to docs/internals/engineering-principles.md -> record wall-clock deltas in telemetry.md. Units never commit regenerated artifacts.

units.title: Shared test-fakes counting instrumentation (pre-wave, sequential)
design_constraints: Land exec/dial/pull-cycle counters ONCE; all Wave-1/2 counting units consume read-only.
Any missing counter shape discovered mid-wave goes to the integrator; never edited on a unit branch.
acceptance_tests: Existing fake-consuming suites green unchanged; counters observable from a sample test.

units.title: B1: lazy jsonschema + heavy-import lint + uv exclusion (sole ci.yml owner)
files: doctor module (jsonschema-importable probe, locate at build)
design_constraints: Owns ALL Wave-1 ci.yml edits (swarm A2); 1.3's smoke and 1.2's lint wiring are integrator lines, not this unit's scope creep.
G5: lazy import moves ImportError to first-validate; add doctor probe 'jsonschema importable' to keep a preflight-time surface.
New lint ships with its synthetic-violation fire test (repo standard).
acceptance_tests: python -X importtime -c "import hpc_agent.meta.campaign.atoms.budget" no longer pulls jsonschema at module scope.
Dev-venv (post rfc3987 purge, operator step) python -X importtime -c "import jsonschema" < 0.5s.
Lint red on a seeded module-level `import jsonschema`.
test_batteries_to_run: new lint fire test in tests/scripts/

units.title: Fast-path prerequisites: --schema into CliShape.args + describe_cache store-guard + parser-truth lint
design_constraints: Move the hand-added `--schema` (parser.py:168-181) into CliShape.args so the bake input is the whole truth (A2); regen at wave integration.
describe_cache.store() refuses when _REGISTRATION_DONE is false (A1 poisoning guard).
New lint forbids add_argument inside _register_from_registry outside _add_standard_args; ci.yml wiring done by the integrator.
NO fast_path_safe flags on describe/find in this unit — that is 2.1's fused scope.
acceptance_tests: `describe <verb> --schema` works from the CliShape-driven parser (no hand add_argument remains).
A store() call under partial registration is refused (unit test).
Lint fires on a synthetic in-registry add_argument.

units.title: B3: PEP-562 lazy __init__ + eager-import smoke contract test (merges LAST; full-CI-matrix gate)
design_constraints: Smoke is a default-tier contract test, not a workflow step (covers 3.10/3.11/Windows legs; swarm A2/A13).
The eager smoke imports EVERY lazily-deferred symbol (guard-can-fire applied to the smoke itself).
__getattr__ serves underscore attrs (operations.py reads hpc_agent._PACKAGE_ROOT at module scope, G4) and raises honest AttributeError/ImportError, never swallows.
mypy TYPE_CHECKING mirror is load-bearing (lint_mirror_ledger).
Merge gate = full CI matrix; merges last in Wave 1 so a red bisects trivially.
acceptance_tests: python -c "import hpc_agent" < 300ms (from ~0.9-1.3s); hpc_agent.__all__ byte-identical.
python -c "import hpc_agent; hpc_agent._PACKAGE_ROOT" green.
Eager smoke red when a deferred name is removed from it (seeded).
enforcement_map_rows: Row 2: Lazy-root eager smoke covers every deferred symbol — fires when a __getattr__-deferred name is missing from the eager import test.

units.title: P5: slot-release flat sub-second wakeup
design_constraints: Slot-cap semantics unchanged; mechanism-only.
Timing assertions xdist-load-tolerant or marked slow (swarm A8).
acceptance_tests: Local harness (HPC_SSH_MAX_CONNECTIONS=1): wakeup lag after release < 0.6s at every hold duration (from ~4-8s). Pins FLIP: tests/infra/test_ssh_slots.py doubling-ladder assertions.

units.title: F5: fetch_task_logs server-side loop with sentinel framing
design_constraints: Remote helper is POSIX sh ONLY (E2); degrade to today's per-file path when the pinned-interpreter probe (agent B's) reports absent.
Sentinel-framed per-file sections (E3): a truncated stream after file k -> files k+1.. read SEVERED, never 'no log/empty log' (positive-evidence-ack, doctrine row L249).
Rebase-first on agent B's landed preamble seam (G3).
acceptance_tests: FxJ log probe = ONE ssh exec (fake transport counts, via Unit 1.0); triage output unchanged; truncated-stream fire test reads severed-per-missing-file.
test_batteries_to_run: LINTS: lint_no_raw_ssh.py, lint_deploy_python_floor.py (if script ships), lint_remote_read_ack.py
enforcement_map_rows: Row 3: Fused-log severed-frame honesty — truncated multi-file stream reads severed-per-missing-file, never empty-logs.

units.title: F6: single pull cycle for both canary samples
design_constraints: The two samples' identities stay distinct (fingerprint admission untouched); -canary family exclusion (rows L271/L373) untouched.
acceptance_tests: Double-canary sample = ONE pull cycle (2 round-trips not 4, counted via Unit 1.0 fakes). Pins: tests/ops/aggregate/test_canary_verify.py pull-count expectations.

units.title: H1: stop_multiplex fused Stop hooks + stdlib prefilter + settings migration + conformance surface
design_constraints: GATED: relay_audit_stop/_contradiction.py is in-flight dirty at memo time — rebase-first on its land.

Multiplexer DISPATCHES; each guard's decision logic stays its own one definition; prefilter = syntactic necessary-conditions only, stdlib-only, never imports hpc_agent, never re-implements a guard predicate.

Fail toward running (technical F1): read sys.stdin.buffer, decode utf-8 errors='replace'; decode failure = fail-OPEN to all guards; per-guard isolation (A's exception still runs B/C and reports A); first-block-wins preserved; one guard blocking never suppresses another's accounting side-effects (relay-audit seat, row L250).
utterance_capture shim routes through the ONE frozen {ts, sha256, text} writer (row L443), adds no field.

Settings migration (technical F2): install_agent_assets removes the THREE legacy Stop entries by exact match in the same write (needles at agent_assets.py:114-116); doctor gains a duplicate-Stop-hook check. Corrected baseline: ~2.4-4.5s/turn, not 5.7-8s.
acceptance_tests: One Stop event = ONE python process; no-op turn exits < 0.4s without importing hpc_agent.
NEW contract test: prefilter-negative => each guard individually no-op (necessary-condition equivalence).
Migration test: settings with the three old entries end with exactly one multiplex entry (absence of old three asserted).
Pins FLIP: tests/_kernel/hooks/test_skill_return_stop_guard.py, test_decision_rendezvous_hooks.py, test_relay_audit_stop.py (invocation shape), tests/cli/test_agent_assets_settings_hook.py.
test_batteries_to_run: CONFORMANCE LANE: pytest -o addopts= -p no:cacheprovider -q src/hpc_agent/conformance/ --harness-adapter hpc_agent.conformance.adapters.claude_code:build
enforcement_map_rows: Row 4: Prefilter necessary-condition equivalence + stdlib-only pin — fires when any input a guard would act on fails the prefilter, or the prefilter imports hpc_agent / re-implements a guard predicate.

units.title: F1: per-task harvest include-fold (metrics+trace+summaries in ONE pull cycle)
design_constraints: .hpc_cmd_sha stays in the pull include (row L521 fires if dropped); canary-family exclusion survives the widened include.
acceptance_tests: Per-task fallback harvest = 1 pull cycle (2 round-trips, counted); all three artifact families present locally. Pins: tests/ops/aggregate/test_flow_gates.py, test_pull_tar_seam.py.

units.title: P4-tier1: combine_wave takes a wave LIST (one exec per burst) — gated on in-flight monitor_flow land
design_constraints: GATED: ops/monitor_flow.py is claimed by in-flight agent B — dispatch only after Wave-0 land; rebase-first.

Per-wave ok/fail granularity in one JSON (honest accounting): a partial batch failure journals per-wave outcomes individually, no silent whole-batch verdict; wave-partial invalidation (row L521 combined_waves->failed_waves) survives batching.
Cluster-side combiner stays the shipped real reducer (one-definition).
E3: sentinel-framed batch output; missing end-sentinel -> per-wave fallback, never parse-and-trust truncation.
acceptance_tests: 10-wave burst tick = 1 combine exec (counted); per-wave ok/fail journaled individually. Pins FLIP: tests/ops/aggregate/test_combine_wave_idempotent.py, tests/cli/test_aggregate.py, tests/ops/monitor/test_flow_announce.py.

units.title: B4+B5+B2 fused: baked hydration for describe/find/capabilities/mcp-serve/parser + narrow fast_path_safe opt-ins (MERGES FIRST in Wave 2)
claims: cold.discovery-verbs-pay-walk-for-baked-data (narrow opt-in per verifier, NOT default flip)
design_constraints: A1: describe/find on the fast path MUST hydrate the catalog from the bake (or run full registration) before answering — never answer from the partial live registry; a fast-path-computed describe row is never store()d without _finalize_composes having run.
A2 prerequisite (unit 1.2) landed: bake = whole truth; no post-registration hand add_argument.
A3: baked answer gated on cached_cli_reshaping_verdict EXTENDED to 'any plugin contributing primitive_modules OR register_cli -> full walk' (dist-signature cache ~5ms is the key).

A4 + doctrine: staleness CONTENT-KEYED on the build fingerprint, never version-string; DIRTY source checkout -> always walk; any hydration mismatch -> walk + byte-identical output, never an error (mirrors _verb_module_map degradation contract).
A5: every open() of baked artifacts passes encoding='utf-8'; help-text arrow round-trip test on Windows CI.
B4-technical: plugin register_cli happens INSIDE the memoized parser build exactly once; memoized surface invoked twice asserts no duplicate registration.
fast_path_safe opt-in set is ENUMERATED with an equality-pin test (reviewed-edit pattern) so a later default-flip turns a test red; byte-identical-output contract vs full path.

Curated-catalog DERIVED property preserved: test_curated_catalog_is_derived_blocks_union_extras keeps firing against the baked answer (row L281); operations.json stays under the generated-merge-driver lockstep (row L507).
acceptance_tests: hpc-agent capabilities < 1.6s; describe/find subprocess wall < 1.6s (from ~5.2-7.1s); mcp-serve time-to-ready < 2.5s (from 7.5-8.7s).
CROSS-MODULE byte-diff cases mandatory: `describe submit-s1`, `find 'submit a batch'`, `describe <verb> --schema` fast-vs-full identical (A1/A2).
Seeded stale bake falls back to walk with identical output; fake plugin env forces walk output identical to today (test_fast_path_plugins case).
Warm in-proc describe stops re-paying ~286ms parser build.
enforcement_map_rows: Row 1: Fast-path opt-in set is enumerated — equality pin on the fast_path_safe verb list; fires when a verb joins without the test moving or the default flips.
Row 5: Stale-bake content-keyed fallback — seeded stale bake -> walk, byte-identical; fires on version-string keying.

units.title: WS-CANARY: liveness split ported to verify_canary (heavy report exactly once, at terminal)
design_constraints: ROUTE THROUGH, never copy: classify_polling/settle (row L244), stamp_watchdog_tick (row L253 — both-loops test extends to the new light loop), remote_activation seeding (rows L282/L287 — join the enumerated-consumer test).

rc-126/127 deterministic-env escalation parity: the LIGHT poll path preserves the deterministic/transient split OR the escalation provably fires at the terminal reporter — fire path: light-path rc-127 stream -> early escalation, never full-budget 'transient' (row L252).
MUST reproduce the vanished-job grace (verify_canary.py:1032-1053).

G1: the single terminal heavy report reuses the e79edc2c-pattern breaker deadline-wait + one retry (SshCircuitOpen carries host+deadline) — a breaker-open at terminal must not lose the verdict of a finished job.
Poll ticks issue only ssh_batch_scheduler_states / read_announcements; consumes infra/cluster_status.py + ops/monitor/announce.py APIs read-only.
Re-check the breaker-livelock narrative against agent B's landed ssh_circuit.py edits (swarm A1).
acceptance_tests: Fake-cluster canary run: heavy reporter invoked EXACTLY once, at terminal (counted in tests/ops/aggregate/test_canary_verify.py ONLY — not 2.5's reporter test).
Fast-failing canary still gets the vanished-job grace verdict.
Breaker-open-at-terminal test: verdict recovered after deadline-wait+retry.
Light-path rc-127 escalates early (fire test).
enforcement_map_rows: Row 6: Light-poll deterministic-env escalation parity — rc-126/127 on the light path escalates early, never rides the budget.

units.title: WS-INPROC: in-process spans for local/decision-only children; ssh + WATCH_VERBS + census waiter keep the subprocess seam
forbidden_files: src/hpc_agent/cli/dispatch.py (entry seam is consumed, not redefined here)
design_constraints: Entry point = the existing MCP in-proc runner seam (_shield_real_stdin + stdout capture), NEVER cli.dispatch.main (technical D1, regression 17243a17); contract test runs a span in-process while a thread blocks on real stdin, asserts server-side stream untouched (Windows CI).

Explicit context object per span (run_id, block, cwd, env-overlay); _record_detached_failure_terminal + heartbeats take parameters, not os.environ (technical D2); lint pins in-process-eligible verbs to no os.chdir / os.environ writes.
In-process eligibility is a DECLARED ENUMERATED list; planted ssh-reaching/WATCH verb in the set turns test_src_subprocess_timeout_discipline red (carve-out encoded, not blanket-ban).

KEEP subprocess: ssh-shelling children (check-preflight, resolve-resources), WATCH_VERBS, and 2.6's per-host census waiter (named here so it cannot be inlined). Tree-kill doctrine rows L273-274; _in_process_deadline is a win32 no-op.
In-process child failure produces the IDENTICAL error envelope + the F14 re-park (a swallowed exception must not convert a park into a silent skip).

Mirror twin (swarm A6): block_drive.py:337 capture path mirrors drive._run_cli_step under lint_mirror_ledger — rebase on landed b7e03e9c, then either de-mirror with ledger update + pinning-test change or make the twin change in drive.py.
In-process dispatch of ops verbs from _kernel/lifecycle routes through the registry/dispatch surface (lint_subject_imports / lint_private_cross_package_imports).
acceptance_tests: block-drive tick chaining 2 local spans spawns 0 subprocesses (from 2); envelope/error capture identical; WATCH_VERBS + check-preflight/resolve-resources still spawn (asserted); measured tick wall -(N-1)x~1.3s.
Planted-violation contract test red.
Pins FLIP: tests/_kernel/lifecycle/test_block_drive.py, test_block_verb_deadlines.py, tests/contracts/test_src_subprocess_timeout_discipline.py.
test_batteries_to_run: cross-consumer: tests/ops/attention/, tests/ops/status/test_snapshot_attention.py, tests/ops/monitor/test_blocks.py, tests/ops/aggregate/test_blocks.py, tests/meta/campaign/test_blocks.py, tests/ops/test_block_gate_and_speculate.py, preflight tests
enforcement_map_rows: Row 7: In-process eligibility carve-out is enumerated — planted ssh/WATCH verb in the in-process set turns the subprocess-discipline contract red.

units.title: F2+F7 transport unit (VERIFY-DURING-BUILD): pull-manifest stat cache + transfer-plane engine routing
design_constraints: FIRST STEP (F7 gate): re-verify the raw-spawn inventory against agent B's LANDED clusters.py/submit_flow.py/host_retarget.py/monitor_flow.py (NOT transport files — swarm A1); abort legs already fixed; commit the verify memo with before/after dial counts.
E1: routed transfer-plane ops use the explicit preamble-free op class from agent B's seam; pinned test asserts byte-level command-line equality (no preamble text) post-routing.

Engine-seam laws extend, never relax (row L278): EngineUnavailable -> one-shot fallthrough; capture=False / streaming tar|ssh pipes NEVER consult the engine; one-shot leg stays on run_capture_bounded — test_transport_ssh_sites_route_through_bounded_runner UPDATED to assert the new route terminates in the bounded runner, never deleted/exempted.

D2 (doctrine, run-13 finding-13 class): manifest cache keys on .hpc_cmd_sha where present; stat only ever decides UNCHANGED — a cmd_sha move MUST evict even when mtime+size match; failures/severed reads never cached (success-only, breaker-invalidated posture); fire test mirrors tests/ops/aggregate/test_flow_stale_mirror.py.
G2: mtime(ns)+size; entries younger than a skew window count dirty; cache file temp+rename, per-run keyed, excluded from include-match; foreign-rows guard test.
acceptance_tests: Warm S2 push <= 4 opens (from ~11-12 at 5 batches), fake-transport dial counts.
Re-aggregate over unchanged tree = stat-walk only; moved .hpc_cmd_sha with unchanged stat is NOT served from cache (fire test); severed read not cached.
F7 verify memo committed; preamble-free byte-equality test green.
Pins FLIP: tests/infra/test_transport_pull.py, test_remote_rsync_fallback.py, tests/ops/aggregate/test_cluster_side_reduce.py; lint_no_raw_ssh.py scope may extend.
enforcement_map_rows: Row 8: Pull-manifest cache content-keyed, success-only, evict-on-sha-move — fires when a moved .hpc_cmd_sha with unchanged stat is served from cache, or a failure/severed read is cached.
Row 9: Engine-seam laws extend to transfer-plane routes — EngineUnavailable falls through, capture=False never consults engine, one-shot leg stays on run_capture_bounded.

units.title: F3: reporter emits rows_observed; min_rows gate computes both sets from ONE report (exclusive owner of cluster_status)
design_constraints: rows_observed exists NOWHERE yet (swarm A4) — real scope = deployed reporter emit side (Python 3.8 floor!) + cluster_status.py parse side + aggregate_flow consumer.
Severed single report reads UNKNOWN for BOTH row sets — never rows_observed=0 -> insufficient (wrap_with_ack/split_ack, row L249).
Version-skew fallback: old reporter (no rows_observed) -> two-call path, exercised by test.
Counts -> verdict still routes through classify (row L244).
Note: ops/aggregate_flow.py is also 1.8's file — cross-wave sequential, rebase on Wave-1 integration.
acceptance_tests: min_rows gate = ONE reporter invocation (counted); old-reporter path takes the two-call fallback; severed report -> UNKNOWN both sets (fire test).
Pin dies: the two-ssh docstring pin at aggregate_flow.py:461 (tests/ops/aggregate/test_flow_gates.py).
enforcement_map_rows: Row 10: Severed single-report reads UNKNOWN for both row sets — never rows_observed=0.

units.title: P3(+P1 folded)+F4: per-host announce census waiter + remote bounded-wait + census-tick fold + dispatcher run-terminal marker (DATA only)
design_constraints: GATED on in-flight monitor_flow.py land; rebase-first.
C1: inotify FORBIDDEN (no cross-node fire on NFS/Lustre) — remote wait = sh poll loop (sleep 1-2s + ls/opendir forcing readdir revalidation); the win is moving the poll remote-side.

C4 (P1 folded into P3): exactly ONE waiter per HOST multiplexing all runs; runs on the engine's persistent asyncssh channel; declared preemptible to the slot limiter; ships as a WATCH_VERB subprocess (on 2.3's KEEP list; tree-killable). Long-poll deadline = current adaptive interval.
C2: a wake is a HINT, never a settle — settle read failing its ack re-enters the wait; announce reads within a settle sequence are sticky to the wake host (login pools x NFS attribute cache).

C3: run-terminal marker is temp-write + same-dir mv (or filename-encoded with census recounting per-task markers as truth); marker name joins the dispatcher/client vocab-equality pin in test_announce.py.
Doctrine row 11: marker wakes, NEVER settles — forged/premature terminal marker wakes the poller, real report reads non-terminal, run keeps watching (fire test, not prose).

Doctrine row 12: dispatcher marker writer lives in the standalone shipped file (Q3, cannot import the package); marker constant in lockstep pin with the control-plane reader (exit_no_output_constant pattern, row L256).
Doctrine row 13: the blocked wait routes through the ONE stamp_watchdog_tick definition (row L253) and the remote bounded-wait command goes through run_capture_bounded (row L274).
Ack-gated severed-vs-empty per run preserved in the fleet census fan-in; F4: census tick derives counts+ids from ONE ls of marker FILENAMES.
P2 (engine idle horizon) NOT built here — only if post-P1 telemetry shows residual (unit 3.2).
acceptance_tests: Fake announce dir: marker write wakes the waiter < 2s with zero intervening dials; fleet-of-3 census = 1 exec/tick (from 3-6).
Forged/premature terminal marker: wake but NO settle (fire test).
Severed-vs-empty distinction per run; watchdog stamped during the blocked wait (assertion, route-through named).
Pins FLIP: tests/ops/monitor/test_flow_adaptive_poll.py, test_announce.py, test_flow_announce.py, test_reconcile_announce.py, tests/ops/monitor/conftest.py fakes (via Unit 1.0).
test_batteries_to_run: tests/execution/mapreduce/test_dispatch_signal.py (-m slow; skips win32 — Linux CI gates)
LINTS: lint_deploy_python_floor.py, lint_schema_versions.py, lint_atomic_durable_writes.py, lint_telemetry_labels.py, lint_remote_read_ack.py
enforcement_map_rows: Row 11: A marker wakes, never settles — forged/premature terminal marker -> real report still computes the verdict.
Row 12: Marker constant lockstep standalone-writer <-> control-plane reader.
Row 13: Long-poll routes through stamp_watchdog_tick + bounded runner.

units.title: WS-SPEC: materialized successor specs (unconditional, run-14 #4 mandate) + dereference-by-run_id (consumption leg iff R3)
claims: run-14 finding #4 (submit-speculate shape failure = proof case)
design_constraints: S1: extend _SUCCESSOR_SPEC_SHAPERS to gated successors — _park composes the COMPLETE proposed successor spec in code (MonitorFlowSpec is NOT in the marker; invocation_argv is agent-known -> shaper + derivation, invocation_argv optional/derivable) and MATERIALIZES it in the run sidecar; the brief carries it. Proceeds WITHOUT ruling.
S2 (iff R3): verbs accept {run_id} and dereference the sidecar — submit-speculate first, then the greenlight path.

Row 14: composer NEVER fills a REQUIRED_CALLER_FIELD — mirror overnight.py's 'cmd_sha is NEVER composed'; a hint lacking human-sourced goal/task_generator REFUSES, never fabricates (seeded fire test).
Row 15: dereference-by-run_id is EXCLUSIVE — simultaneous inline spec is refused, never merged (else the run-14 #4 class survives through the back door).
Row 16: sidecar spec sha-stamped at park; consumption recomputes and refuses on drift; if attestation-shaped it routes through state/attestation.py::bind (row L141 — no fifth recompute-and-compare).
Derived invocation_argv joins the CODE_DERIVED_FIELDS partition treatment (row L266 — bound, not copied).
Materialized spec must round-trip through the extra='forbid' models (SubmitS3Spec) — fixes the latent overnight auto-advance wedge.
Cross-wave file order on SKILL.md + block_drive: 3.1 -> 4a -> 5.1.
acceptance_tests: Park at S2-terminal materializes a VALIDATING SubmitS3Spec file in the sidecar.
Overnight auto-advance integration test crosses S2->S3 without agent-authored JSON.
submit-speculate --run-id <id> (no inline spec) fires correctly; --run-id + inline spec REFUSED.
Field-less hint refuses; edited-after-park sidecar refused on sha drift.

Pins FLIP: tests/contracts/test_spec_hint_completeness.py, tests/_kernel/lifecycle/test_block_drive_specs.py, tests/ops/test_block_gate_and_speculate.py, tests/_wire/ schema pins (invocation_argv optional).
test_batteries_to_run: CHECKS/LINTS: build_schemas.py --check, lint_wire_suffix.py, lint_schema_reachability.py, lint_skill_command_sync.py, lint_atomic_durable_writes.py, primitive doc templates
enforcement_map_rows: Row 14: Successor-spec composer never fills a REQUIRED_CALLER_FIELD — field-less hint refuses, never fabricates.
Row 15: run_id dereference is exclusive — inline spec alongside --run-id refused, never merged.
Row 16: Park-time sha bind, consumption-time recompute — edited-after-park sidecar spec refused; routes through attestation.bind if attestation-shaped.

units.title: P2: cadence-aware engine idle horizon (CONDITIONAL on post-P1 telemetry)
design_constraints: Build ONLY if Wave-2 telemetry shows residual reconnect-per-poll after the long-poll ships.
Engine stays opt-in/fallback (row L278 untouched).
acceptance_tests: Declared 300s cadence keeps the connection across 2 polls (0 reconnects, from 1/poll); one-shot callers still recycled at 120s. Pins: tests/infra/test_ssh_engine.py IDLE_CLOSE sweep.

units.title: WS-DAEMON: warm daemon serves all surfaces (iff R4; opt-in HPC_CLI_DAEMON=1 first)
design_constraints: RULING R4 gates; depends on WS-BAKE (fast boot).

D3 (doctrine): every daemon-dispatched journal append routes through the ONE infra/io.append_jsonl_line seam AND the RPC reply is written only after the seam's fsync returns (a lost post-ok:true greenlight is the worst journal corruption).

B2-technical: v1 SERIALIZES requests (single worker queue); per-request {cwd, env-allowlist} applied from the RPC; refuse calls whose critical env (HPC_JOURNAL_DIR, HPC_SSH_*) cannot be applied. Concurrency is explicitly OUT of this program (request-context accessor is v2).

B1-technical: handshake on FULL build fingerprint (full_version(), incl. git sha); refuse-and-fall-back + daemon SELF-EXIT on mismatch; launch via python -m (never the console-script .exe — WinError-32); release skill gains a stop-daemon step; doctor gains a fingerprint-skewed-daemon check.
B3-technical: pipe/mutex name embeds user + fingerprint; second instance detects and exits; argparse SystemExit caught per-RPC.
G6: dist-signature re-checked per RPC (~5ms) — editable-plugin verdict staleness.
env_actor resolves from the CALL's env through the one HPC_ACTOR seam (rows L438-441).
Hard fallback = guard-integrity law: daemon-unreachable degrades to inline, byte-identical — a dead daemon never means a silently skipped guard. Three-stream shield discipline (row L486).
acceptance_tests: Daemon up: hpc-agent read-decisions --help < 200ms end-to-end; daemon down: inline path byte-identical.
Kill daemon immediately after an append-decision reply: record durable on disk (D3 fire path).
Two sequential calls from different cwds land in DIFFERENT journals; actor from call env.
Version-skew test refuses, falls back, daemon self-exits.
enforcement_map_rows: Row 17: Daemon append: one seam + reply-after-fsync — daemon killed post-reply leaves the record durable.
Row 18: Daemon per-call cwd/actor — cross-cwd calls hit different journals; actor resolves from call env through the one env_actor seam.

units.title: L1: fused commit+advance (block-drive --approve; rendezvous guard demoted to a backstop that provably still fires)
design_constraints: Mechanism-only, no ruling: the human still utters the y; fusion removes the agent's mechanical second call.

Row 19: --approve ROUTES THROUGH the one append_decision definition — every existing gate (bare-ack refusal, human-authorship on REQUIRED_CALLER_FIELDS, code-derived-field refusal row L266, token-exact prior-nudge row L288, unlock authorship row L299) fires identically on the fused path (fire test: fused path refuses each thing append-decision refuses).
Row 20: the demoted rendezvous Stop guard STILL FIRES on an unjournaled advance (verify-a-guard-can-fire applied to the demotion; else the demotion deleted the guard).
Sequences AFTER 3.1 (same files: block_drive, SKILL.md); 4b runs after 4a.
Consumes 3.1's materialized proposed spec.
acceptance_tests: One --approve call journals the y (record shape byte-compatible with append-decision) AND returns the next parked brief; rendezvous guard no longer fires on the fused path; backstop fires on a seeded unjournaled advance.
Pins FLIP: tests/_kernel/hooks/test_decision_rendezvous_hooks.py, tests/ops/decision/test_overnight_wiring.py.
test_batteries_to_run: LINTS: lint_decision_content.py, lint_skill_command_sync.py, conformance lane, primitive doc + regen
enforcement_map_rows: Row 19: --approve routes through the one append_decision — every append-decision refusal fires identically on the fused path.
Row 20: Demoted rendezvous backstop still fires on an unjournaled advance.

units.title: L2: worker-exit park (one-definition _park, second seat) + L3: code-fired speculative canary at S1 resolved-park (iff R2)
design_constraints: Runs AFTER 4a. L2 needs no ruling; L3 needs R2.
Row 21: _park one-definition-two-seats with the import-location contract test (scope_gate/notebook_gate blessed pattern).

Row 22 (L3, iff R2): speculative fire reuses the #249 TTL/budget-of-1/breaker-invalidation cache as its ONE speculation-state definition (no second cache); stop_after_canary pinned UNSTRIPPABLE on the speculative path (fire: main array unreachable); nudge/moved-cmd_sha orphans it; required ambiguities -> skip (same rule as the skill).
Bank L2's win as '3 agent round-trips/run' (bogus 2-4s figure dropped; cursor-resume already shipped).
acceptance_tests: Detached worker exit leaves a pending-decision marker + brief (no driver tick needed); wait-detached returns {outcome, brief, relay, next_verb}; _park import-location contract test green.
L3: S1 resolved-park fires the speculative canary in code; nudge (cmd_sha move) orphans it; ambiguous walk skips it; journal untouched by the speculative fire.
Severance tests skip on win32 — Linux CI gates.
enforcement_map_rows: Row 21: _park one-definition-two-seats import-location pin.
Row 22: Speculative canary: one TTL-cache definition, stop_after_canary unstrippable, nudge orphans.

units.title: L5: standing-consent collapse at all four gate sites (iff R1, WITH the D1 typed-minting bar)
design_constraints: RULING R1 gates. D1 minting bar is a constraint OF the grant: a bare y mints greenlight-only, byte-identical to today; minting a standing consent requires a typed phrase naming the boundary set; the minting utterance faces the typed-authorship check.

Row 23: all four sites route through the ONE assert_greenlit_or_consented gate (route-through enumeration test — no site re-derives consent locally); an anomaly/needs_decision terminator PARKS under a live consent (fire test — consent covers only the healthy chain); the spend-envelope disclosure is CODE-rendered from structured evidence (row L265 — the LLM never composes the spend line).
consume_boundary_under_consent stays the one 'live consent' definition; every consumption ledgered; revise-resolved/nudge revokes (cmd_sha binding).
Sequences after 3.1/4a on SKILL.md + submit_blocks; 5.3 sequences AFTER this unit (swarm A12).
acceptance_tests: Healthy-submit integration: ONE typed consent at S1 (expires_at+caps+cmd_sha) carries S2/S3/S4 with a ledgered ConsumptionOutcome each; bare y -> greenlight only, byte-identical (fire test); anomaly terminator still parks; nudge revokes.
Pins FLIP: tests/ops/decision/test_overnight_wiring.py, tests/ops/test_block_gate_and_speculate.py, gate-site tests asserting strict assert_greenlit_target at s2/s4/aggregate-run.
enforcement_map_rows: Row 23: Consent gate — route-through at all four sites; anomaly parks under live consent; spend line code-rendered; bare y mints no consent (typed-authorship minting bar).

units.title: Breaker class-dependent cooldown (R6 — DEFER-recommended; build only after a classifier-telemetry run cycle)
design_constraints: RULING R6 gates; recommendation is DEFER one run cycle for classifier false-positive telemetry.

If granted: ~30-60s-to-first-probe ONLY for the preamble-degradation/load class (d3b1915f classifier, fires at cycle >=2); ban/connection-refused classes keep the FULL ladder; single bare probe, no session storm; must not regress row L523 (connection success never resets reopen_cycles).
REBASE on in-flight ssh_circuit.py (dirty at memo time).
acceptance_tests: Load-class open (classifier-flagged, cycle >=2) probes at ~45s; ban-class keeps the full ladder (both directions asserted); reopen_cycles never reset by success.

units.title: Speculation-eligibility law + S2-push/S4-pull prefire (iff R5; sequences AFTER 5.1)
design_constraints: RULING R5 gates; AFTER 5.1 (both flip pins in test_block_gate_and_speculate.py and alter gate semantics at the same sites — swarm A12).

The lint mechanizes ONLY the non-scheduler-mutating leg (SideEffect kind taxonomy); idempotent+content-keyed are ASSERTED semantics -> each eligible verb requires an ENUMERATED double-run/byte-identical fire test to join the flag set (row-L287 enumeration pattern).

Row 24 pin: speculative execution NEVER appends a decision record and NEVER records a block terminal — speculative S2 push leaves the journal byte-identical; greenlight stays the only path to scheduler mutation + journal advancement; _spec_sha move orphans prefired work.
Wire S2-push first, S4-pull second; speculative work runs low-priority under the slot limiter.
New lint lands with its synthetic-violation fire test.
acceptance_tests: Planted non-eligible verb with the flag -> lint red; per-verb double-run byte-identical tests enumerated; speculative S2 push: journal byte-identical (fire test); _spec_sha move orphans.
enforcement_map_rows: Row 24: Speculation-eligible = flag + kind-taxonomy lint + enumerated per-verb idempotency fire test + journal-byte-identical speculative execution.

units.title: WS-AGENT: cluster-side campaign agent DESIGN DOC + digest schema (docs only; build is a follow-on program)
design_constraints: SHIP the real hpc_agent reducer on-node (one-definition — never a stdlib reimplementation of combine); lifecycle kill at campaign-complete; install ladder degrades to today's polling; informed by WS-PUSH telemetry.
Doctrine mandate (i): env-fingerprint 'attestation' in the digest routes through state/attestation.py::bind/reduce or stops calling itself an attestation (row L141).

Doctrine mandate (ii): the digest is DATA — control-plane classify_polling/settle still compute every verdict; a cluster-computed verdict is never trusted directly (rows L244/L249 extended; marker-never-settles posture restated from 2.6/row 11).
acceptance_tests: Design doc carries both doctrine mandates verbatim + the digest schema + the degrade ladder.
enforcement_map_rows: Row 11 (restated): the WS-AGENT digest is data — a marker/digest wakes or informs, never settles; the control plane computes every verdict.

rulings_needed.recommendation: grant S4+aggregate-run unconditionally; S2/S3 contingent on code-rendered spend-envelope disclosure

rulings_needed.recommendation: DEFER one run cycle for classifier telemetry, then grant narrowly (single bare probe; ban class keeps the full ladder)
